action.reasoning: Observation: PyTorch Geometric and its dependencies (scatter, sparse, cluster) are broken in this environment and cause segmentation faults. Analysis: To avoid these broken C++ extensions, I will implement a Dense SchNet from scratch using only standard PyTorch operations. Since QM9 molecules are small (max 29 atoms), dense matrix operations will be extremely fast and robust. Decision: Write model.py with DenseSchNet, update train.py and predict.py to use it, train for 2 epochs, and submit an evaluation to get a baseline.
